root_text.an4.73:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, asappuriso yo hoti parassa avaṇṇo taṁ apuṭṭhopi pātu karoti, ko pana vādo puṭṭhassa.
root_text.an4.73:1.4: Puṭṭho kho pana pañhābhinīto ahāpetvā alambitvā paripūraṁ vitthārena parassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an4.73:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, asappuriso yo hoti parassa vaṇṇo taṁ puṭṭhopi na pātu karoti, ko pana vādo apuṭṭhassa.
root_text.an4.73:2.2: Puṭṭho kho pana pañhābhinīto hāpetvā lambitvā aparipūraṁ avitthārena parassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an4.73:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, asappuriso yo hoti attano avaṇṇo taṁ puṭṭhopi na pātu karoti, ko pana vādo apuṭṭhassa.
root_text.an4.73:3.2: Puṭṭho kho pana pañhābhinīto hāpetvā lambitvā aparipūraṁ avitthārena attano avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an4.73:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, asappuriso yo hoti attano vaṇṇo taṁ apuṭṭhopi pātu karoti, ko pana vādo puṭṭhassa.
root_text.an4.73:4.2: Puṭṭho kho pana pañhābhinīto ahāpetvā alambitvā paripūraṁ vitthārena attano vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an4.73:4.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato asappuriso veditabbo.
root_text.an4.73:5.1: Catūhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato sappuriso veditabbo.
root_text.an4.73:5.3: Idha, bhikkhave, sappuriso yo hoti parassa avaṇṇo taṁ puṭṭhopi na pātu karoti, ko pana vādo apuṭṭhassa.
root_text.an4.73:5.4: Puṭṭho kho pana pañhābhinīto hāpetvā lambitvā aparipūraṁ avitthārena parassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an4.73:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, sappuriso yo hoti parassa vaṇṇo taṁ apuṭṭhopi pātu karoti, ko pana vādo puṭṭhassa.
root_text.an4.73:6.2: Puṭṭho kho pana pañhābhinīto ahāpetvā alambitvā paripūraṁ vitthārena parassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an4.73:7.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, sappuriso yo hoti attano avaṇṇo taṁ apuṭṭhopi pātu karoti, ko pana vādo puṭṭhassa.
root_text.an4.73:7.2: Puṭṭho kho pana pañhābhinīto ahāpetvā alambitvā paripūraṁ vitthārena attano avaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an4.73:8.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, sappuriso yo hoti attano vaṇṇo taṁ puṭṭhopi na pātu karoti, ko pana vādo apuṭṭhassa.
root_text.an4.73:8.2: Puṭṭho kho pana pañhābhinīto hāpetvā lambitvā aparipūraṁ avitthārena attano vaṇṇaṁ bhāsitā hoti.
root_text.an4.73:8.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato sappuriso veditabbo.
root_text.an4.73:9.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, vadhukā yaññadeva rattiṁ vā divaṁ vā ānītā hoti, tāvadevassā tibbaṁ hirottappaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ hoti sassuyāpi sasurepi sāmikepi antamaso dāsakammakaraporisesu.
root_text.an4.73:9.2: Sā aparena samayena saṁvāsamanvāya vissāsamanvāya sassumpi sasurampi sāmikampi evamāha:
root_text.an4.73:9.4: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco bhikkhu yaññadeva rattiṁ vā divaṁ vā agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajito hoti, tāvadevassa tibbaṁ hirottappaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ hoti bhikkhūsu bhikkhunīsu upāsakesu upāsikāsu antamaso ārāmikasamaṇuddesesu.
root_text.an4.73:9.5: So aparena samayena saṁvāsamanvāya vissāsamanvāya ācariyampi upajjhāyampi evamāha:

translation_text.an4.73:1.1: “Mendicants, an untrue person can be known by four qualities.
translation_text.an4.73:1.3: To start with, an untrue person speaks ill of another even when not asked, let alone when asked.
translation_text.an4.73:1.4: But when summoned for questioning they speak ill of another in full detail, not leaving anything out.
translation_text.an4.73:1.5: That’s how to know that this is an untrue person.
translation_text.an4.73:2.1: Furthermore, an untrue person doesn’t speak well of another even when asked, let alone when not asked.
translation_text.an4.73:2.2: But when summoned for questioning they speak well of another without giving the full details, leaving many things out.
translation_text.an4.73:2.3: That’s how to know that this is an untrue person.
translation_text.an4.73:3.1: Furthermore, an untrue person doesn’t speak ill of themselves even when asked, let alone when not asked.
translation_text.an4.73:3.2: But when summoned for questioning they speak ill of themselves without giving the full details, leaving many things out.
translation_text.an4.73:3.3: That’s how to know that this is an untrue person.
translation_text.an4.73:4.1: Furthermore, an untrue person speaks well of themselves even when not asked, let alone when asked.
translation_text.an4.73:4.2: But when summoned for questioning they speak well of themselves in full detail, not leaving anything out.
translation_text.an4.73:4.3: That’s how to know that this is an untrue person.
translation_text.an4.73:4.4: An untrue person can be known by these four qualities.
translation_text.an4.73:5.1: A true person can be known by four qualities.
translation_text.an4.73:5.3: To start with, a true person doesn’t speak ill of another even when asked, let alone when not asked.
translation_text.an4.73:5.4: But when summoned for questioning they speak ill of another without giving the full details, leaving many things out.
translation_text.an4.73:5.5: That’s how to know that this is a true person.
translation_text.an4.73:6.1: Furthermore, a true person speaks well of another even when not asked, let alone when asked.
translation_text.an4.73:6.2: But when summoned for questioning they speak well of another in full detail, not leaving anything out.
translation_text.an4.73:6.3: That’s how to know that this is a true person.
translation_text.an4.73:7.1: Furthermore, a true person speaks ill of themselves even when not asked, let alone when asked.
translation_text.an4.73:7.2: But when led on by questions they speak ill of themselves in full detail, not leaving anything out.
translation_text.an4.73:7.3: That’s how to know that this is a true person.
translation_text.an4.73:8.1: Furthermore, a true person doesn’t speak well of themselves even when asked, let alone when not asked.
translation_text.an4.73:8.2: But when summoned for questioning they speak well of themselves without giving the full details, leaving many things out.
translation_text.an4.73:8.3: That’s how to know that this is a true person.
translation_text.an4.73:8.4: A true person can be known by these four qualities.
translation_text.an4.73:9.1: It’s like a bride on the day or night she’s first brought to her husband’s home. Right away she sets up a keen sense of conscience and prudence for her mother and father in law, her husband, and even the bondservants, workers, and staff.
translation_text.an4.73:9.2: But after some time, because of living together and familiarity, she’ll even say to her mother and father in law, or to her husband:
translation_text.an4.73:9.4: In the same way, on the day or night a mendicant first goes forth from the lay life to homelessness, right away they set up a keen sense of conscience and prudence for the monks, nuns, laymen, and laywomen, and even the monastery workers and novices.
translation_text.an4.73:9.5: But after some time, because of living together and familiarity, they’ll even say to their tutor or mentor:
translation_text.an4.73:9.7: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an4.73:9.8: ‘We will live with hearts like that of a newly wedded bride.’
